storylets.title: {=home_settle_food3_title}Three days of grain
storylets.setup: {=home_settle_food3_setup}The quartermaster counts sacks twice, and the tally doesn't change. Whatever the writ says, the writ is behind the barrels.

storylets.title: {=home_settle_food5_title}Short rations
storylets.setup: {=home_settle_food5_setup}The bread is thinner than yesterday. No one says it. You notice it when you break your own, and you say nothing either.

storylets.title: {=home_settle_wounded_title}The surgeon's tent
storylets.setup: {=home_settle_wounded_setup}You help carry a man who won't stop bleeding. Your lord is in a smaller tent, behind a curtain, being quiet about his own hurt. You were seen pulling your weight.

storylets.title: {=home_settle_stores_title}Counting barrels
storylets.setup: {=home_settle_stores_setup}The quartermaster and the castle stewards square off over an oat-count that doesn't match the writ. Your own rations aren't touched — yet.

storylets.title: {=home_settle_autumn_title}The first bite of autumn
storylets.setup: {=home_settle_autumn_setup}A chill settles into the bones of the walls. Someone sends out for more firewood. Your breath makes a plume on the morning air, and your hands take longer to warm.

storylets.title: {=home_settle_winter_title}Snow on the walls
storylets.setup: {=home_settle_winter_setup}The snow has settled into the crenellations. You are sent to help chip ice from the water-barrel and your hands ache after. The cold gets into the gear too; the old sergeant warns you to oil everything, twice.

storylets.title: {=home_settle_lowloy_title}Eyes in the market
storylets.setup: {=home_settle_lowloy_setup}The market-folk look through you, not at you. Someone spits after your patrol passes. Your sergeant tells you not to notice, so you don't.

storylets.title: {=home_settle_threat_title}The last raid is still warm
storylets.setup: {=home_settle_threat_setup}You ride past a burned byre on the approach. Inside the walls, the talk is louder, uglier. Men grip their cups a little harder when they speak of it.

storylets.title: {=home_settle_mercy_title}A word for the wounded
storylets.setup: {=home_settle_mercy_setup}Your lord stops in the surgeon's tent longer than the rounds require. He has a short word for each man. Someone who can't answer him is told he is expected to live, and the lie kindly told.

storylets.title: {=home_settle_valor_title}Into the yard at dawn
storylets.setup: {=home_settle_valor_setup}Before the watch-horn, your lord is already in the practice yard, working through forms with a dented training-sword. Two squires stand watching like men who have learned not to interrupt.

storylets.title: {=home_settle_chaplain_title}The chaplain passes through
storylets.setup: {=home_settle_chaplain_setup}An old cleric moves from fire to fire, saying what he says over bowed heads. He pauses at yours, touches your shoulder, moves on.

storylets.title: {=home_settle_siege_title}Preparing for worse
storylets.setup: {=home_settle_siege_setup}Orders come down hourly. Arrows are bundled, water-butts filled, the keep's stores moved up a floor. No one asks you about your day.
